page: Amazon Web Services c7gn.2xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an ARM64-based AWS Graviton processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz. The CPU is built on the ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 architecture and includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results place the c7gn.2xlarge in the average tier for most operations. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1532.39, while the multi-core score reaches 12245.01. In memory bandwidth tests, it achieves 774.36 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 150.89 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. It demonstrates solid capabilities in data processing, with a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 24.19 GB/sec and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 42.4 GB/sec. However, the server exhibits weaker performance in PassMark CPU tests, scoring 6153.63 overall and 1105.51 in encryption tests.

This server is optimized for network-intensive compute workloads, web serving, and database operations, as evidenced by its static web serving throughput of 16.28 GB/sec and 13.54 million Redis SET operations per second. It is also capable of handling lightweight machine learning tasks, processing 420.54 tokens per second for a small 135M LLM model, though performance drops significantly to 15.04 tokens per second for prompt processing on a medium 7B model. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached EBS volumes, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of memory, a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. In benchmark evaluations, the c7gn.2xlarge demonstrates average performance across most compute and memory tests, achieving a stress-ng multi-core score of 12245.01 and a static web serving throughput of 16.28 GB/sec. However, it shows weaker results in PassMark CPU encryption and medium-sized LLM prompt processing. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for network-heavy, compute-focused workloads such as web serving, database operations, and software compilation, provided that external storage is acceptable.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Graviton cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for web serving and databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 8 Graviton cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring dedicated Graviton cores and high network bandwidth for web serving and database workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARM64 Graviton physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average performance in web serving, software compilation, and database operations
Weaker performance in PassMark CPU encryption and larger LLM workloads
No local storage, requiring network-attached EBS volumes
